---
title: ""
author: "Giorgio Arcara"
date: ""
#format: revealjs
format:
  beamer:
    theme: metropolis
    keep-tex: false
incremental: false
header-includes:
  \setbeamerfont{title}{size=\large}
  \usepackage{hyperref}
  \setbeamertemplate{footline}[frame number]
---

<!-- the section below is shown only if rendering is beamer from latex. (and format:revealjs is commmented) -->

::: {.content-visible when-format="beamer"}
```{=latex}
% TITLE SLIDES
\title{{\fontsize{15pt}{16pt}\selectfont Applied Psychometrics}:\\ studying subclinical psychopathologies\\ \vspace{1.5em} \raggedright 
\emph{3.2 - Selecting Tests: Psychometric Properties}}
\author{Giorgio Arcara,\\ University of Padua \\ IRCCS San Camillo, Venice}

\titlegraphic{

\vspace*{7cm}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{Figures/LogoSanCamilloIRCCS_Unipd_alpha.png}
\begin{center}
\vspace{-2.2em}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{Figures/CC_license_3_0.png}
\end{center}
}
\date{2026}
\maketitle
```
:::

## A test is a measurement tool
\centering

![](Figures/Test_measurement_tool.png){width="70%"}

## A tentative list of properties to consider
\small

- Validity  \pause

- Reliability  \pause

- Clinical Applicability (e.g. test duration) \pause

- Normative data  \pause

- Discriminative ability \pause

- Thresholds for change over time \pause

## Three intersecting aspects
\centering
![](Figures/Selecting_Tests.png){width="70%"}


## Psychometric Properties and Goals

Sometimes we may want to select not the best tool (in terms of properties), if another is more suited to our goals.

## Psychometric Properties and Practical Aspects

A very good test could not be the more popular or your favourite one
\pause

_Beware of this reasoning! you may be stuck with suboptimal tests_
